parts.text:
Main Scene: A massive, imposing citadel, known locally as 'Dragon's Tooth,' dominates the frame, perched precariously on a jagged, misty mountain peak. Its grey, weather-beaten stone walls rise almost organically from the rock, blending into the craggy landscape. A single, ancient stone bridge, narrow and arching, is the only visible approach, spanning a deep chasm. The sun is just beginning to break through the morning mist, casting long, dramatic shadows and highlighting the texture of the ancient stones.

Key Elements: The citadel itself is a marvel of medieval engineering, featuring several distinct towers of varying heights and diameters. The central keep is a massive, square structure with battlements adorned with flickering torches, even in the early morning. Two formidable gatehouses, each with a raised portcullis and heavy oak doors, protect the main entrance. Flags bearing the insignia of a roaring dragon unfurl lazily from tall flagpoles atop the highest turrets. Below the main structure, carved into the mountainside, are smaller ancillary buildings, their stone roofs partially covered with snow and moss. A winding cobblestone path leads from the main gate down towards the bridge.

Humorous/Charming Details: Perched precariously on the very edge of one of the highest battlements, a mischievous raven is attempting to peck at a loose stone, seemingly trying to dislodge it. On a ledge just below, a very fluffy white cat with one ear notched is meticulously grooming itself, completely oblivious to the raven's antics. A tiny, almost imperceptible detail shows a miniature knight figurine carved into one of the stone bridge's arch supports, wearing a comical oversized helmet. A small, forgotten broom leans against a guard tower, its bristles well-worn from diligent sweeping.

Environment Details: The mountain peak is rugged and inhospitable, with sparse, hardy pine trees clinging to the steepest slopes. Below the bridge, the chasm is filled with swirling mist, hinting at the immense drop. Patches of lingering snow cling to shaded areas and ledges, even as the sun begins to warm the air. Wisps of fog still curl around the base of the towers and through the crenelations. Lichen and dark green moss cling to every available surface of the ancient stone walls, adding to their timeless appearance. The air feels crisp and cold.

Background: In the far distance, beyond the chasm and the lower mountain ranges, a vast, dark forest stretches towards the horizon, its canopy a patchwork of deep greens and browns. A thin, silvery river can be glimpsed winding its way through the valley far below the citadel. The sky is a dramatic canvas of breaking clouds, with hints of orange and soft pink where the sun is rising, gradually transitioning to a lighter, clearer blue higher up.
